tagline: Yesterday's good news, bright and early.

stories.headline: Goalkeeper's Tearful Plea Brings Mother Home for World Cup
stories.summary: Cape Verde's Vozinha emotionally wished for his mother during a historic shutout; U.S. officials fast-tracked her visa so she could watch him play.

stories.headline: From Acid Attack Survivor to World Cup Hero
stories.summary: Yoane Wissa, nearly blinded by a 2021 acid attack, scores a crucial equalizer for DR Congo against Portugal in stunning triumph of resilience.

stories.headline: Scotland Fan Gifts £900 Ticket to Scammed Supporter
stories.summary: John MacLean, a Scot living in the US for 26 years, gifted his £900 World Cup ticket to 23-year-old Jack Rundell after learning he'd been scammed, deciding in 30 seconds to give it away for free.

stories.headline: ALS Patient Speaks 99% Clearly Through Brain Implant at Home
stories.summary: A man with ALS used a brain-computer interface independently for nearly two years, logging 3,800 hours speaking and working with remarkable accuracy.

stories.headline: Girl Scouts Build Raised Garden for Woman in Care Home
stories.summary: A Girl Scout troop used cookie-sale earnings to design and build a raised garden bed so an isolated woman could grow flowers again.

stories.headline: Nine-Year-Olds and Seniors Celebrate a Decade of Friendship
stories.summary: Nine-year-olds from École Tuxedo Park and seniors in their 90s marked a decade of friendship, regularly bonding over bocce and building genuine intergenerational connection in Winnipeg.

stories.headline: One-Time CRISPR Treatment Stops Hereditary Disease in Its Tracks
stories.summary: A landmark gene-editing therapy reduced angioedema attacks by 87%, with nearly two-thirds of patients attack-free—a watershed moment for genetic medicine.

stories.headline: Belgrano Wins First Title in 121 Years
stories.summary: Nicolás Fernández scored twice in the final minutes to give Belgrano its first championship in 121 years, defeating River Plate 3-2 in an unforgettable Argentine final.

stories.headline: Beavers Return After 400 Years, End a City's Decade of Flooding
stories.summary: Ealing, West London reintroduced a family of five beavers after 400 years extinct, whose dams ended a decade of flooding plaguing the neighborhood for the first time.

stories.headline: Two Teenagers Help Elderly Woman After Mobility Scooter Fall
stories.summary: Two Year 9 students from Sanders Drapers School rushed to help an elderly woman who fell from her mobility scooter, then walked her safely home with genuine care.

stories.headline: Endangered Piping Plovers Reach Record Nesting Numbers
stories.summary: The Great Lakes now host a record 90 piping plover nesting pairs this summer—a stunning recovery from just 12 pairs in 1990.

stories.headline: Ghana Teenager Scores Stunning World Cup Winner in Stoppage Time
stories.summary: Twenty-year-old Caleb Yirenkyi scored his first international goal in the fifth minute of stoppage time to lift Ghana past Panama 1-0 in their World Cup opener.

stories.headline: Solar Device Quietly Produces Drinking Water Through the Night
stories.summary: Rice University engineers created a membrane-free solar desalination system that generates fresh water 24 hours a day without batteries or external power.
